# motionforge

> A deterministic, browser-native video scene engine. The canonical video is a serializable JSON scene document; rendering is a pure function of (scene, frame). Agents author and patch scene JSON; the engine validates, lays out, and renders it.

## Mental model

```txt
scene JSON + frame -> parseScene -> evaluateScene (keyframes) -> layoutScene (boxes) -> renderStill (Canvas2D) -> pixels
```

- Time is integer frames at `scene.fps`, never wall-clock seconds.
- A node is active for `[from, from + duration)` in its parent's local timeline; children's clocks restart when the parent appears.
- Keyframe `frame` values are local to the node and must be strictly increasing (validated). Numeric values interpolate; color strings (`#hex`, `rgb()`, `rgba()`) interpolate in RGBA space; transform lists (`translate`/`scale`/`rotate`) tween when function sequences and units match; other strings step. Easing: `linear`, `easeIn`, `easeOut`, `easeInOut`, `cubic-bezier(x1, y1, x2, y2)` (x1/x2 in [0,1]), or `spring(bounce)` (bounce in [0,1); may overshoot mid-tween).
- Node `id`s are unique across the scene by contract — patch and diff scenes by id.

## Hard rules

- Never use wall-clock time or unseeded randomness in anything that feeds rendering.
- Only use style properties from the support matrix (docs/scene-format.md). Unknown properties fail validation — this is intentional; do not work around it.
- Prefer data keyframes over imperative per-frame code.
- After editing a scene, re-validate with `validateScene()` and read the errors; they state the path, the problem, and the fix.

## Minimal valid scene (JSON)

```json
{
  "schemaVersion": 0,
  "width": 1080,
  "height": 1920,
  "fps": 30,
  "duration": 90,
  "assets": {},
  "nodes": [
    {
      "id": "bg",
      "type": "div",
      "style": { "width": "100%", "height": "100%", "backgroundColor": "#101820" }
    },
    {
      "id": "title",
      "type": "text",
      "text": "Hello motionforge",
      "style": {
        "position": "absolute",
        "left": 64,
        "right": 64,
        "top": 800,
        "fontSize": 72,
        "color": "#ffffff",
        "textAlign": "center"
      },
      "animations": [
        {
          "kind": "keyframes",
          "property": "opacity",
          "frames": [
            { "frame": 0, "value": 0 },
            { "frame": 12, "value": 1, "easing": "easeOut" }
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

Node types: `div`, `text` (requires `text`), `img` (requires `assetId` pointing at `scene.assets`; draws with `objectFit`/`objectPosition`), `video` (requires `assetId`; optional `videoStartTime` trim in seconds and `playbackRate` multiplier — `sourceTime = videoStartTime + (localFrame / fps) * playbackRate`, last frame holds past clip end), `audio` (requires `assetId`; optional `audioStartTime` trim in seconds and `volume` 0..1; no style/children/animations — placement is `from`/`duration`; mixed into the exported MP4's audio track, overlaps sum). Lengths: number = px, `"50%"` = of parent, `"64px"`.

Asset workflow: loading is the only async phase. `const assets = await resolveAssets(scene)` (from `@motionforge/renderer-canvas2d`); for scenes with video nodes also `await prepareFrame(scene, frame, assets)` per frame (no-op otherwise; `exportVideo`/`renderFrameSequence` call it automatically); then `renderStill(ctx, scene, frame, { assets })`. Rendering `img`/`video` without resolved/staged media throws; `exportVideo()` resolves assets itself when not given them. Font assets register under their asset id — reference them as `fontFamily: "<asset id>"`, one asset per family+weight, without `fontWeight`. Call `disposeAssets(assets)` to release video decoders.

Styles implemented today: `width`, `height`, `minWidth`/`minHeight`/`maxWidth`/`maxHeight` (min wins over max), `position` (`relative`/`absolute`), `left`/`top`/`right`/`bottom`, `inset`, `padding`, `margin` (single value, outer spacing), `display: "flex"`, `flexDirection`, `gap`, `justifyContent` (`flex-start`/`center`/`flex-end`/`space-between`), `alignItems` (`flex-start`/`center`/`flex-end`/`stretch`), `backgroundColor`, `background` (solid or `linear-gradient` with any stop count, `deg`/`to <side>` directions), `borderRadius`, `overflow` (`hidden` clips children, follows borderRadius), `opacity`, `transform` (`translate`/`scale`/`rotate`), `transformOrigin`, `fontSize`, `fontFamily`, `fontWeight`, `fontStyle`, `color`, `textAlign`, `textShadow`, `textStroke` (`"<width> <color>"`, stroke before fill), `textBackgroundColor` plus `textBackgroundPadding`/`X`/`Y`/`Radius` (measured per-line pill backgrounds before stroke/fill), `lineHeight` (unitless = multiplier of fontSize), `letterSpacing`, `objectFit` (`fill`/`contain`/`cover`/`none`/`scale-down`, img and video nodes), `objectPosition`, `border` (`"<width> solid <color>"`, inside the box, follows borderRadius), `boxShadow` (`"<x> <y> [blur] <color>"`, requires a background fill; no inset/spread), `filter` (chain of `brightness`/`contrast`/`saturate`/`grayscale`/`sepia`/`invert`/`opacity` with number or `%`, `hue-rotate(<deg>)`, `blur(<px>)` — applies per node draw, not to the composited subtree), `zIndex` (integer; reorders sibling painting, never layout). Every validated style property is implemented.

Text is multi-line: explicit `\n` always breaks; words wrap to the node's box width using real font metrics; lines are spaced by `lineHeight` and the block is centered vertically in the box. For caption pills, prefer `textBackgroundColor` on the `text` node instead of wrapper divs with hand-sized widths.

## Example validation errors

```txt
nodes.1.id: Duplicate node id "title". Node ids must be unique across the scene so tools can patch and diff nodes reliably.
nodes.0.style: Unsupported style property. motionforge v0 supports a curated CSS-like subset; move unsupported behavior into supported transforms, layout, or custom draw nodes.
nodes.2.assetId: img nodes require an assetId that points at scene.assets.
```

## APIs

- `@motionforge/schema`: `parseScene(json)` (throws `SceneValidationError`), `validateScene(json)` (returns `{ ok, scene | errors }`), `sceneJsonSchema()` (JSON Schema draft-07; also shipped as `scene.schema.json`).
- `@motionforge/core`: `composition()`, `div()`, `text()`, `img()`, `video()` builders → `.toJSON()`; `evaluateScene(scene, frame)`, `layoutScene(resolved)`.
- `@motionforge/renderer-canvas2d`: `resolveAssets(scene)` → `ResolvedAssets` (async, once per scene); `prepareFrame(scene, frame, assets)` (async, per frame, stages video frames); `renderStill(ctx2d, scene, frame, { assets })`; `disposeAssets(assets)`; `computeObjectFit()` and `videoSourceTime()` for geometry/timing math.
- `@motionforge/presets`: pure helpers compiling intent to scene data — `popIn`/`fadeUp`/`slideIn(direction)`/`pulse(options)` → keyframe arrays; `tiktokCaptions(words, { fps, highlightIndices })` and `karaokeCaptions(words, { fps })` turn ASR word timestamps (`{ word, startMs, endMs }[]`) into timed caption nodes. Prefer these over hand-writing animation JSON.
- `@motionforge/export`: `detectExportCapability()`; `exportVideo({ scene, onProgress?, startFrame?, endFrame?, signal? })` → `{ blob, codec, audioCodec, totalFrames }` — in-browser MP4 via WebCodecs + mediabunny with the scene's audio nodes mixed into an audio track (gate on `detectExportCapability().videoEncoder`); `renderFrameSequence({ scene, context, onFrame })` — the underlying deterministic frame loop with µs timestamps; `mixAudioSegments()` and `collectAudioPlacements()` for audio math.

## Docs

- [Scene format spec and style support matrix](docs/scene-format.md): the canonical contract.
- [JSON Schema for scenes](packages/schema/scene.schema.json): validate structurally without running code.
- [M0 roadmap](docs/m0-roadmap.md): what is in and out of scope right now.
- [Testing strategy](docs/testing-strategy.md): how changes must be verified.
- [Contributing and coding standards](CONTRIBUTING.md): determinism rules for the render path.
